title: Health, Health Care, and Aging
gist: A RAND health-topics index page whose recent-research teasers surface a few actionable signals: widespread veteran psychedelic use amid unclear VA policy, one-in-five youth turning to AI chatbots for mental health advice, China filling the global-health vacuum as the US retreats, and US biosecurity gaps around crop and engineered pathogens.

insights.claim: An estimated 4.8 million US veterans have used psilocybin mushrooms, LSD, or MDMA in their lifetime, and about half are unsure whether to discuss that use with a VA doctor while VA policy remains unclear despite federal research investment.
insights.speaker: RAND Corporation
insights.so_what: A large, policy-ambiguous patient population signals a maturing catalyst for psychedelic-therapy developers as VA guidance is forced to catch up.
insights.quote: An estimated 4.8 million U.S. veterans have used psilocybin mushrooms, LSD, or MDMA in their lifetime.
insights.locator: Nearly 5 Million U.S. Veterans Have Used Psilocybin

insights.claim: Nearly 1 in 5 US adolescents and young adults use AI chatbots for mental health advice.
insights.speaker: RAND Corporation
insights.so_what: Youth mental-health demand is already flowing to consumer AI chatbots, a fast-growing adoption vector and a looming regulatory and liability battleground.
insights.quote: Nearly 1 in 5 U.S. Adolescents and Young Adults Use AI Chatbots for Mental Health Advice

insights.claim: China is shifting its global health engagement from the Belt and Road Initiative toward the Global Development Initiative just as the United States retreats toward an America First approach, a divergence with implications for US global influence.
insights.speaker: RAND Corporation
insights.so_what: China filling a US-vacated global-health role expands its soft-power and pharma/supply footprint in developing markets, a geopolitical trend to track.

insights.claim: The US biosecurity system is poorly prepared for new threats, with not-yet-present natural crop diseases capable of causing billions of dollars in losses and deliberately delivered engineered pathogens posing far worse risk, underscored by the Texas screwworm cases.
insights.speaker: RAND Corporation
insights.so_what: Identified biosecurity gaps point to policy and spending tailwinds for agricultural-biosecurity, diagnostics, and pathogen-surveillance providers.
insights.quote: Naturally occurring crop diseases not yet present could cause billions of dollars in losses if introduced here.
insights.locator: The Texas Screwworm Cases Are a Wake-Up Call

insights.claim: Among young adult drinkers in California, alcohol-free and low-alcohol beverage consumption was associated with lower social drinking motives and a higher likelihood of alcohol abstention one year later.
insights.speaker: RAND Corporation
insights.so_what: Evidence that no/low-alcohol products correlate with subsequent abstention supports the structural growth thesis for the non-alcoholic beverage category.

insights.claim: In Medicaid, prior authorization requirements are being studied for their effect on the duration and discontinuation of buprenorphine treatment for opioid use disorder.
insights.speaker: RAND Corporation
insights.so_what: Prior-authorization friction on addiction treatment is an access lever that affects utilization for treatment-drug makers and managed-care plans.
insights.locator: Medicaid Prior Authorization and Discontinuation of Buprenorphine
